chunks.title: United States — HQC Standard Finalized (Expected)
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2027–2027
Title: HQC Standard Finalized (Expected)
Description: Final standard incorporating HQC algorithm expected to be published approximately one year after draft.

chunks.title: United States — NSS Acquisitions CNSA 2.0 Required
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: NSS Acquisitions CNSA 2.0 Required
Description: New National Security Systems acquisitions must be CNSA 2.0 compliant starting January 2027.

chunks.title: United States — 112-bit Security Deprecated
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: 112-bit Security Deprecated
Description: Algorithms at 112-bit security strength (e.g., RSA-2048, DH-2048, ECDH P-256) deprecated per NIST IR 8547 (draft). Disallowed in federal systems after 2035. Systems must migrate to 128-bit minimum (AES-256, SHA-384+) or approved PQC algorithms.

chunks.title: United States — CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - Network/Signing
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - Network/Signing
Description: Exclusively use CNSA 2.0 for software/firmware signing and traditional networking equipment.

chunks.title: United States — NSS Quantum Resistant Majority
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2031–2031
Title: NSS Quantum Resistant Majority
Description: Vast majority of cryptography in National Security Systems should be quantum resistant.

chunks.title: United States — CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - OS/Web/Cloud
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2033–2033
Title: CNSA 2.0 Exclusive - OS/Web/Cloud
Description: Exclusively use CNSA 2.0 for web browsers/servers, cloud services, operating systems, and large PKI systems.

chunks.title: United States — Full CNSA 2.0 Transition Complete
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Full CNSA 2.0 Transition Complete
Description: Complete transition to CNSA 2.0 algorithms for all National Security Systems (NSS).

chunks.title: United States — CISA PQC Product Category List Publication
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2025–2025
Title: CISA PQC Product Category List Publication
Description: CISA released initial list of product categories supporting PQC per Executive Order. List developed with ATARC covering data management, networking, and enterprise endpoint security. Products must be generally available, FIPS-compliant, and interoperable.

chunks.title: United States — CNSA 1.0 Compliance Deadline for NSS
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2025–2025
Title: CNSA 1.0 Compliance Deadline for NSS
Description: All existing National Security Systems must be CNSA 1.0 compliant  or submit waiver request by end of year. Systems not compliant have 6 months to achieve CNSA 2.0 compliance or request waiver.

chunks.title: International — PKI Consortium PQC Conference 2025 Conclusions
chunks.content:
Country: International
Organization: Public Key Infrastructure Consortium
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PKI Consortium PQC Conference 2025 Conclusions
Description: World's largest PQC conference concluded in Kuala Lumpur with 2500+ delegates from 30+ countries. Issued urgent global call for immediate action on PQC migration. Next conference announced for Germany 2026.

chunks.title: United States — NIST PQC Algorithms in Windows Platform
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: Microsoft Corporation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: NIST PQC Algorithms in Windows Platform
Description: Microsoft announced general availability of NIST post-quantum cryptography algorithms ML-KEM and ML-DSA in Windows 11 and Windows Server 2025 via November 2025 update  enabling enterprise PQC adoption.

chunks.title: United States — US Post-Quantum Root of Trust Service Launch
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: SEALSQ Corp
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: US Post-Quantum Root of Trust Service Launch
Description: SEALSQ launched US-based Post-Quantum Root of Trust service for enterprises and government agencies aligned with NIST PQC standards and NSA CNSA 2.0 framework integrating with Quantum Shield QS7001 hardware.

chunks.title: International — ML-KEM Embedded Secure Element Breakthrough
chunks.content:
Country: International
Organization: Samsung System LSI and Thales
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2026
Title: ML-KEM Embedded Secure Element Breakthrough
Description: Samsung System LSI and Thales announce breakthrough in embedded security bringing ML-KEM quantum-resistant capabilities to tiny secure elements in electronic devices. First commercial PQC implementation for IoT and embedded systems.

chunks.title: International — RFC 9794 PQC Hybrid Terminology
chunks.content:
Country: International
Organization: Internet Engineering Task Force
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2025–2025
Title: RFC 9794 PQC Hybrid Terminology
Description: IETF publishes RFC 9794 Terminology for Post-Quantum Traditional Hybrid Schemes. Standardizes definitions for hybrid PQC/classical cryptographic schemes. Over 20 IETF drafts now reference this standard.

chunks.title: International — PQC Inventory Workbook Published
chunks.content:
Country: International
Organization: Post-Quantum Cryptography Coalition
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: PQC Inventory Workbook Published
Description: PQCC publishes PQC Inventory Workbook to assist organizations in creating centralized inventory for tracking cryptographic migration efforts. Follows May 2025 PQC Migration Roadmap. Founded by IBM, Microsoft, MITRE, PQShield, SandboxAQ.

chunks.title: Global — Helios Quantum Computer Commercial Launch
chunks.content:
Country: Global
Organization: Quantinuum
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Helios Quantum Computer Commercial Launch
Description: Quantinuum launches Helios commercial quantum computer with 98 all-to-all connected qubits and industry-leading fidelity (99.9975% single-qubit 99.921% two-qubit gate fidelity). Designed to enable Generative Quantum AI.

chunks.title: United States — National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: United States Congress
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act
Description: Bipartisan legislation introduced to extend National Quantum Initiative by 5 years to December 2034. Authorizes $85M/yr for NIST quantum centers, creates 5 NSF Multidisciplinary Centers for Quantum Research. Endorsed by IBM Microsoft Google IonQ.

chunks.title: G7 — Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
chunks.content:
Country: G7
Organization: G7 Cyber Expert Group
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
Description: G7 Cyber Expert Group (chaired by US Treasury and Bank of England) releases Advancing a Coordinated Roadmap for PQC Transition in Financial Sector. Recommends critical financial systems quantum-safe by 2030-2032, non-critical by 2035.

chunks.title: United States — Federal PQC Procurement Guidance
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Federal PQC Procurement Guidance
Description: CISA issues federal buying guidance per EO 14306 directing agencies to acquire only PQC-capable products in widely available categories: cloud services, collaboration tools, browsers, and endpoint security. Networking and identity management still transitioning.

chunks.title: Global — Post-Quantum Security Top Priority
chunks.content:
Country: Global
Organization: Ethereum Foundation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Post-Quantum Security Top Priority
Description: Ethereum Foundation elevates post-quantum security to top strategic priority. Forms dedicated PQ team, launches $2M in bounties (Poseidon Prize $1M and Proximity Prize $1M), and begins bi-weekly PQ All Core Devs calls February 2026.

chunks.title: Singapore — Helios Quantum Computer Singapore Deployment
chunks.content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Quantinuum and Singapore National Quantum Office
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Helios Quantum Computer Singapore Deployment
Description: Quantinuum partners with Singapore National Quantum Office to deploy Helios quantum computer (98 qubits) first deployment outside US. R&D and Operations Centre established at one-north technology district.

chunks.title: International — ETSI/IQC QSC Conference 2026
chunks.content:
Country: International
Organization: ETSI and University of Waterloo IQC
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: ETSI/IQC QSC Conference 2026
Description: 11th ETSI/IQC Quantum Safe Cryptography Conference announced for Ottawa, hosted by Carleton University June 16-18. Topics include PQC deployments, QKD, migration challenges, and cryptographic agility.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Ordinance
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Legislative Council of Hong Kong
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Ordinance
Description: Protection of Critical Infrastructure (Computer System) Ordinance passed by Legislative Council on March 19 2025. Effective January 1 2026. Covers 8 sectors: energy IT banking air transport land transport maritime healthcare and telecommunications.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — HKMA Fintech Adoption Report: PQC Commitment
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: HKMA Fintech Adoption Report: PQC Commitment
Description: HKMA publishes Fintech Adoption: Progress and Future Directions report (Jan 2025 Tech Maturity Stock-take of 56 authorized institutions). Finds only 7% of Hong Kong banks engaged with quantum computing. Commits to promoting post-quantum cryptography as primary quantum-safety mechanism. Plans to highlight importance of quantum safety and practical PQC adoption for the financial sector.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Fintech 2030 Strategy: PQC Resilience Pillar
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Fintech 2030 Strategy: PQC Resilience Pillar
Description: HKMA unveils Fintech 2030 strategy at Hong Kong FinTech Week 2025. The Resilience pillar explicitly commits to driving industry readiness for post-quantum cryptography and building quantum-safe infrastructure for secured financial services. Over 40 initiatives under four pillars: Data (D), AI (A), Resilience (R), and Tokenisation (T).

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Fintech Promotion Blueprint: Quantum Preparedness Index
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Fintech Promotion Blueprint: Quantum Preparedness Index
Description: HKMA unveils Fintech Promotion Blueprint including Quantum Preparedness Index as flagship project to assess banking sector PQC readiness and set measurable migration targets. The Index will guide institutions through PQC transition journey with special focus on supporting small and medium-sized banks.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Financial Sector PQC Planning
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Phase | Phase: Discovery
Period: 2026–2028
Title: Financial Sector PQC Planning
Description: Banks develop quantum preparedness plans and cryptographic inventories per HKMA Fintech 2030 Quantum Preparedness Index. Industry consultation on PQC adoption guidelines underway.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Fintech Promotion Blueprint Published
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Fintech Promotion Blueprint Published
Description: HKMA publishes Fintech Promotion Blueprint (From Adoption to Advancement) on February 3, 2026, formalizing the Quantum Preparedness Index as a flagship initiative to measure banking sector PQC readiness and set measurable quantum-safe migration targets.

chunks.title: Singapore — NQSN+ Launched
chunks.content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Infocomm Media Development Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2023–2023
Title: NQSN+ Launched
Description: Deputy PM Heng Swee Keat launches National Quantum-Safe Network Plus (NQSN+) at ATxSG 2023. Businesses can integrate QKD and PQC solutions through NQSN+ operators. IMDA publishes Singapore first local QKD network standard.

chunks.title: Singapore — Cross-Border PQC Experiment
chunks.content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Monetary Authority of Singapore and Banque de France
Type: Milestone | Phase: Testing
Period: 2024–2024
Title: Cross-Border PQC Experiment
Description: MAS and Banque de France conduct groundbreaking cross-border post-quantum cryptography experiment to enhance communication security between central banks using PQC algorithms.

chunks.title: India — National Quantum-Safe Roadmap Published
chunks.content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: National Quantum-Safe Roadmap Published
Description: DST Task Force publishes comprehensive phased roadmap for PQC migration. CII foundations by 2027 high-priority migration by 2028 full CII quantum-safe by 2029 nationwide adoption by 2033. Mandates Cryptographic Bill of Materials (CBOM) submissions from FY 2027-28.

chunks.title: India — CII PQC Foundations Deadline
chunks.content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: CII PQC Foundations Deadline
Description: Critical Information Infrastructure in defense power telecom space and core government must have PQC adoption foundations in place. Mandatory CBOM submissions begin. Testing labs (TEC STQC BIS) to be operational by December 2026.

chunks.title: India — National PQC Migration
chunks.content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2027–2033
Title: National PQC Migration
Description: Phased migration: high-priority CII systems by 2028 full CII quantum-safe by 2029 all sectors by 2033. Hybrid PQC plus QKD recommended for high-assurance applications. Quantum-safe requirements integrated into procurement.

chunks.title: India — Full Nationwide PQC Adoption
chunks.content:
Country: India
Organization: Department of Science and Technology National Quantum Mission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2033–2033
Title: Full Nationwide PQC Adoption
Description: Target completion for PQC migration across all sectors nationwide under National Quantum Mission roadmap.

chunks.title: Japan — Government PQC Transition Deadline
chunks.content:
Country: Japan
Organization: National Cyber Command Office
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Government PQC Transition Deadline
Description: Japan National Cyber Command Office sets 2035 as deadline for government agencies to complete PQC transition. Aligns with US UK and Canada timelines. Detailed implementation roadmap planned for FY2026. Recommends cryptographic agility and hybrid schemes during transition.

chunks.title: Malaysia — National PQC Migration Plan Published
chunks.content:
Country: Malaysia
Organization: National Cyber Security Agency of Malaysia
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National PQC Migration Plan Published
Description: Malaysia Ministry of Digital and NACSA publish National PQC Migration Plan (2025-2030) and launch PQC Sandbox 2025 proof-of-concept program at PKI Consortium PQC Conference in Kuala Lumpur. Positions Malaysia as ASEAN PQC leader.

chunks.title: Malaysia — National PQC Migration
chunks.content:
Country: Malaysia
Organization: National Cyber Security Agency of Malaysia
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2030
Title: National PQC Migration
Description: Phased national PQC migration with proof-of-concept sandbox program. First ASEAN nation to publish a comprehensive national PQC migration roadmap.

chunks.title: United Arab Emirates — NIST Additional Signatures Round 2 Contributions
chunks.content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Technology Innovation Institute
Type: Milestone | Phase: Standardization
Period: 2024–2024
Title: NIST Additional Signatures Round 2 Contributions
Description: TII Cryptography Research Center contributes to 6 of 14 NIST Round 2 additional digital signature schemes: LESS Mirath PERK RYDE SDitH and SQISign. TII also contributed to FN-DSA (FALCON) standardization as FIPS 206.

chunks.title: United Arab Emirates — Dubai PQC Guideline Launched
chunks.content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Dubai Electronic Security Center
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Dubai PQC Guideline Launched
Description: DESC launches Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline at GISEC Global 2025 to prepare Dubai digital infrastructure against quantum threats. Also launches Zero Trust Assessment Tool and Ethaq Plus digital trust platform.

chunks.title: United Arab Emirates — Quantum-Secure Communications Testbed
chunks.content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: Abu Dhabi Global Market and Technology Innovation Institute
Type: Milestone | Phase: POC
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Quantum-Secure Communications Testbed
Description: UAE first quantum-secure communications testbed: 3-node QKD network deployed across Abu Dhabi Global Market financial centre. Joint initiative by ADGM TII Hub71 and ASPIRE in live commercial environment.

chunks.title: United Arab Emirates — National Encryption Policy Approved
chunks.content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: UAE Cybersecurity Council
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National Encryption Policy Approved
Description: UAE Cybersecurity Council approves National Encryption Policy and Executive Regulation at CyberQ 2025 summit. Mandates all government entities develop PQC transition plans submit migration roadmaps by 2026 and maintain automated cryptographic asset inventories with crypto agility by design.

chunks.title: United Arab Emirates — National PQC Migration Program
chunks.content:
Country: United Arab Emirates
Organization: UAE Cybersecurity Council
Type: Phase | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2030
Title: National PQC Migration Program
Description: QuantumGate partnership drives large-scale PQC implementation across National Information Assurance Program National Cybersecurity Index Platform and National Post-Quantum Migration Program. Crypto Discovery Tool and QSphere VPN deployed across government.

chunks.title: Saudi Arabia — ECC-2:2024 Cybersecurity Controls
chunks.content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: National Cybersecurity Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Regulation
Period: 2024–2024
Title: ECC-2:2024 Cybersecurity Controls
Description: NCA releases Essential Cybersecurity Controls 2024 (ECC-2:2024) with expanded cryptography domain covering encryption strength key management secure protocols and lifecycle governance. New NCA Regulations grant enforcement authority.

chunks.title: Saudi Arabia — Quantum Economy Landscape Report
chunks.content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: Center for the Fourth Industrial Revolution
Type: Milestone | Phase: Policy
Period: 2024–2024
Title: Quantum Economy Landscape Report
Description: Saudi C4IR publishes Quantum Economy Landscape in Saudi Arabia report. First country to pilot WEF Quantum Economy Blueprint. National quantum strategy with $200M initial budget aligned with Vision 2030.

chunks.title: Saudi Arabia — First Quantum Computer in KSA
chunks.content:
Country: Saudi Arabia
Organization: Saudi Aramco and Pasqal
Type: Milestone | Phase: Research
Period: 2025–2025
Title: First Quantum Computer in KSA
Description: Aramco and Pasqal deploy Saudi Arabia first quantum computer (200 qubits neutral atom) at Aramco data center. Region first quantum computer dedicated to industrial applications. Plans to upgrade to hybrid analog-digital mode.

chunks.title: Bahrain — National Quantum-Safe Deployment
chunks.content:
Country: Bahrain
Organization: National Cyber Security Centre
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2025–2025
Title: National Quantum-Safe Deployment
Description: Bahrain NCSC partners with SandboxAQ to deploy AQtive Guard AI-powered cryptographic security platform across 60+ government ministries. One of world first large-scale national commitments to quantum-safe cybersecurity.

chunks.title: Jordan — Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
chunks.content:
Country: Jordan
Organization: Central Bank of Jordan
Type: Milestone | Phase: Guidance
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Financial Sector PQC Roadmap
Description: Central Bank of Jordan publishes sector-wide roadmap for transitioning banks to quantum-resistant encryption. Phased approach: cryptographic asset cataloging risk-based prioritization governance integration sandbox testing and full transition.

chunks.title: Global — Vitalik Buterin Publishes PQ Defense Roadmap
chunks.content:
Country: Global
Organization: Ethereum Foundation
Type: Milestone | Phase: Migration
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Vitalik Buterin Publishes PQ Defense Roadmap
Description: Detailed roadmap identifying 4 quantum-vulnerable areas (BLS consensus, KZG commitments, ECDSA accounts, ZK proofs). Proposes hash-based signatures (Winternitz), STARK aggregation, and EIP-8141 native account abstraction for PQ signature schemes. Targets Hegota upgrade H2 2026.

chunks.title: United States — Google Cloud ML-KEM Default and Chrome MTC Program
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: Google LLC
Type: Milestone | Phase: Industry
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Google Cloud ML-KEM Default and Chrome MTC Program
Description: Google deploys ML-KEM by default for all Cloud network encryption. Announces Chrome Merkle Tree Certificates (MTC) program with Cloudflare for PQ-safe HTTPS replacing X.509 chains with compact Merkle proofs. Chrome Quantum-resistant Root Store (CQRS) planned Q3 2027.

chunks.title: Germany — Hybrid TLS Required for Classified Communications
chunks.content:
Country: Germany
Organization: Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Hybrid TLS Required for Classified Communications
Description: BSI TR-02102-2 (January 2025 edition) mandates hybrid TLS combining classical (ECDH) and PQC (ML-KEM) for classified communications by 2025. Non-classified systems should adopt hybrid TLS as soon as practical.

chunks.title: Germany — Hybrid IPsec and SSH Required
chunks.content:
Country: Germany
Organization: Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2026–2026
Title: Hybrid IPsec and SSH Required
Description: BSI TR-02102-3 and TR-02102-4 (January 2025 editions) mandate hybrid IPsec (IKEv2 with ML-KEM) and hybrid SSH (with ML-KEM) for sensitive communications by 2026. Aligns with IETF ipsecme and curdle working group drafts.

chunks.title: Germany — Full PQC Transition — Standalone Algorithms Required
chunks.content:
Country: Germany
Organization: Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: Full PQC Transition — Standalone Algorithms Required
Description: BSI TR-02102-1 (January 2025 edition) sets 2035 as the target for requiring standalone PQC algorithms. Hybrid configurations (classical + PQC) are permitted from 2030 onward; standalone PQC replaces hybrid requirement by 2035 for all sensitive systems.

chunks.title: European Union — Cryptographic Inventory Mandate
chunks.content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2025–2025
Title: Cryptographic Inventory Mandate
Description: EU PQC Recommendation (April 2024) requires all EU public sector entities and operators of essential services to complete a comprehensive cryptographic asset inventory by end of 2025 as the first phase of the coordinated PQC transition roadmap.

chunks.title: European Union — High-Priority System Migration Begins
chunks.content:
Country: European Union
Organization: European Commission
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: High-Priority System Migration Begins
Description: EU PQC Recommendation (April 2024) sets 2027 as the target for beginning PQC migration of high-priority systems including those handling critical data with long confidentiality requirements. Full migration of critical infrastructure and high-risk systems must be completed by 2030.

chunks.title: Singapore — Financial Sector PQC Migration Plans Due
chunks.content:
Country: Singapore
Organization: Monetary Authority of Singapore
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2028–2028
Title: Financial Sector PQC Migration Plans Due
Description: MAS Quantum Advisory (2024) requires regulated financial institutions to develop and submit detailed PQC migration plans by 2028 following initial risk assessments (2026) and cryptographic inventories. Aligned with CSA Singapore quantum-safe migration framework.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — PQC Readiness Assessment Required
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2027–2027
Title: PQC Readiness Assessment Required
Description: HKMA Fintech 2030 Quantum Preparedness framework requires all regulated banks to complete a formal quantum preparedness assessment by 2027 covering cryptographic inventory CBOM and preliminary migration roadmap submission.

chunks.title: Hong Kong — Financial Sector PQC Migration Complete
chunks.content:
Country: Hong Kong
Organization: Hong Kong Monetary Authority
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2030–2030
Title: Financial Sector PQC Migration Complete
Description: HKMA Fintech 2030 framework targets completion of PQC migration for all regulated financial institutions by 2030 aligned with international APAC financial regulator timelines including MAS Singapore and BIS guidance.

chunks.title: United States — 112-bit Security Algorithms Fully Disallowed
chunks.content:
Country: United States
Organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology
Type: Milestone | Phase: Deadline
Period: 2035–2035
Title: 112-bit Security Algorithms Fully Disallowed
Description: NIST IR 8547 sets 2035 as the disallowance deadline for all algorithms at 112-bit security strength including RSA-2048 DH-2048 and ECDH P-256 in federal systems. Distinct from 2030 deprecation (no new use): by 2035 existing use is also prohibited. Systems must migrate to 128-bit minimum or approved PQC algorithms.

chunks.title: Korean Post-Quantum Cryptography Competition Final Results
chunks.content:
Reference: KpqC-Competition-Results
Title: Korean Post-Quantum Cryptography Competition Final Results
Description: Korea selects HAETAE and AIMer (signatures) plus SMAUG-T and NTRU+ (KEMs) as national PQC standards. Target standardization by 2029 migration by 2035.
Type: Algorithm | Status: Final Results
Authors: KISA; Ministry of Science and ICT; NIS Korea
Algorithm Family: Lattice-based; MPC-based
Security Levels: L1,L3,L5
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: Government; Telecom; Finance; Critical Infrastructure
Region: South Korea; APAC
Published: 2025-01-31 | Updated: 2025-01-31

chunks.content:
Reference: NIST-FIPS140-3-IG-PQC
Title: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
Description: Updated FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance adding self-test requirements for FIPS 203/204/205 PQC algorithms and new guidance for Key Encapsulation Mechanisms.

Authors: NIST CMVP

chunks.content:
Reference: 3GPP-PQC-Study-2025
Title: Study on Preparing for Transition to Post Quantum Cryptography in 3GPP
Description: 3GPP study on integrating standalone or hybrid PQC algorithms into existing 5G security protocols. References TR 33.841 PQC Study. Covers hybrid PQC for TLS, IPSec, IKEv2 in 5G.
Type: Research/Report | Status: Study Item Approved
Authors: 3GPP SA3

Security Levels: L3,L5
Migration Urgency: High

chunks.content:
Reference: liboqs-v0.15.0
Title: Open Quantum Safe liboqs Library v0.15.0
Description: Latest liboqs release with updated PQC algorithm implementations including NIST-standardized ML-KEM ML-DSA and SLH-DSA. Part of Linux Foundation PQCA.

Authors: Open Quantum Safe; Linux Foundation PQCA

chunks.content:

Title: TPM 2.0 Post-Quantum Cryptography Library Specification

Authors: Trusted Computing Group
Algorithm Family: Lattice; Hash-based
Security Levels: L1,L3,L5

chunks.title: Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Standard (SLH-DSA)
chunks.content:
Reference: FIPS 205
Title: Stateless Hash-Based Digital Signature Standard (SLH-DSA)

chunks.title: FFT over NTRU-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm (FN-DSA)
chunks.content:

Title: FFT over NTRU-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm (FN-DSA)

chunks.title: Hamming Quasi-Cyclic (HQC) Algorithm Specification
chunks.content:
Reference: HQC Specification
Title: Hamming Quasi-Cyclic (HQC) Algorithm Specification

chunks.title: Importing External Pre-Shared Keys (PSKs) for TLS 1.3
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC-9258
Title: Importing External Pre-Shared Keys (PSKs) for TLS 1.3
Description: RFC 9258 defines an external PSK importer interface for TLS 1.3 (RFC 8446). It allows QKD-derived keys delivered via ETSI GS QKD 014 to be bound to a specific KDF and hash algorithm and imported as a TLS 1.3 pre-shared key — enabling QKD integration without any TLS protocol modifications.

Title: Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions (Revision 1)
Description: NIST SP 800-108 Rev.1 specifies key derivation functions (KDFs) based on pseudorandom functions including counter mode feedback mode and double-pipeline mode using HMAC or CMAC. Directly implemented in PKCS#11 v3.0 as CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF and used in HSM-based QKD key derivation pipelines.

chunks.title: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
chunks.content:
Reference: NIST-SP-800-56C-R2
Title: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
Description: NIST SP 800-56C Rev.2 specifies one-step and extraction-then-expansion (EtE) key derivation methods for key-establishment schemes. The EtE approach supports hybrid shared secrets combining classical DH or PQC KEM output with a QKD-sourced entropy value — directly applicable to quantum-hybrid key exchange architectures.

chunks.title: PKCS #11 Cryptographic Token Interface Standard Version 3.0
chunks.content:
Reference: PKCS11-V3-OASIS
Title: PKCS #11 Cryptographic Token Interface Standard Version 3.0
Description: PKCS#11 v3.0 is the HSM programming interface standard. It adds CKM_HKDF_DERIVE and CKM_HKDF_KEY_GEN mechanisms for TLS 1.3 and CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF / FEEDBACK_KDF / DOUBLE_PIPELINE_KDF for NIST SP 800-108 derivation. Essential for HSM-based QKD integration workflows where QKD secrets are imported as non-extractable keys and used to derive session keys. Superseded by PKCS#11 v3.1 and v3.2.
Type: Protocol | Status: OASIS Standard
Authors: OASIS PKCS11 Technical Committee
Algorithm Family: Symmetric; QKD
Security Levels: N/A
Migration Urgency: High

chunks.title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.1 (OASIS Standard)
chunks.content:
Reference: PKCS11-V31-OASIS
Title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.1 (OASIS Standard)
Description: PKCS#11 v3.1 adds LMS and HSS stateful hash-based signature mechanisms (CKM_HSS_KEY_PAIR_GEN, CKM_HSS) for quantum-resistant code signing and firmware verification. Also adds EdDSA mechanisms (CKM_EDDSA) and incremental MAC operations. First PKCS#11 version with any PQC algorithm support.
Type: Protocol | Status: OASIS Standard
Authors: OASIS PKCS11 Technical Committee
Algorithm Family: Hash-based

chunks.title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.2 (Committee Specification Draft)
chunks.content:
Reference: PKCS11-V32-OASIS
Title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.2 (Committee Specification Draft)
Description: PKCS#11 v3.2 adds post-quantum cryptography mechanisms: ML-KEM (CKM_ML_KEM 0x00000017, CKM_ML_KEM_KEY_PAIR_GEN 0x0000000f), ML-DSA (CKM_ML_DSA 0x0000001d, CKM_ML_DSA_KEY_PAIR_GEN 0x0000001c, CKM_HASH_ML_DSA_*), and SLH-DSA (CKM_SLH_DSA, CKM_SLH_DSA_KEY_PAIR_GEN). Introduces C_EncapsulateKey() and C_DecapsulateKey() functions for KEM operations. New key types: CKK_ML_KEM (0x49), CKK_ML_DSA (0x4a), CKK_SLH_DSA (0x4b). New attributes: CKA_PARAMETER_SET, CKA_ENCAPSULATE, CKA_DECAPSULATE, CKA_SEED.
Type: Protocol | Status: Committee Specification Draft
Authors: OASIS PKCS11 Technical Committee
Algorithm Family: Lattice; Hash-based
Security Levels: L1,L2,L3,L5

chunks.content:
Reference: BIP-340
Title: BIP-340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1
Description: Defines 64-byte Schnorr signatures for secp256k1 with provable security, batch verification, and key aggregation (MuSig2). Required foundation for Taproot (BIP-341). BIP-360 (P2QRH) targets Taproot's Schnorr key-spend path as the primary quantum vulnerability to replace with post-quantum signatures.
Type: Specification | Status: Final
Authors: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Tim Ruffing (Bitcoin Core)
Algorithm Family: Classical
Security Levels: 128-bit

chunks.title: BIP-341: Taproot: SegWit Version 1 Spending Rules
chunks.content:
Reference: BIP-341
Title: BIP-341: Taproot: SegWit Version 1 Spending Rules
Description: Activates Taproot on Bitcoin (block 709632). Defines SegWit v1 spending with a Schnorr key-spend path and Merklized Abstract Syntax Tree (MAST) script-spend path. BIP-360 (P2QRH) specifically removes the key-spend path as quantum-vulnerable under Shor's algorithm and proposes a SegWit v3 (bc1r...) replacement.
Type: Standard | Status: Final
Authors: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Anthony Towns (Bitcoin Core)

chunks.title: Bitcoin BIP-360: Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
chunks.content:
Reference: Bitcoin-BIP360-P2QRH
Title: Bitcoin BIP-360: Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
Description: Proposes a new Bitcoin output type (SegWit v3, bc1r...) using post-quantum signatures via a soft fork. Removes the quantum-vulnerable key-spend path from Taproot. Exact PQC algorithms to be standardized in a companion BIP; ML-DSA and FALCON-512 are leading candidates under community discussion. Authored by Hunter Beast to address the HNFL risk to Bitcoin public keys.

Title: EIP-7702: Set Code for EOA
Description: Allows EOAs to set executable code via signed authorisation tuples (Pectra hard fork). Provides a bridge for PQC migration: a delegated smart contract can verify ML-DSA or FALCON signatures on behalf of the EOA, enabling quantum-safe signing without full account migration. Note: EIP-7702 authorisation tuples are themselves signed with secp256k1; quantum resistance is provided by the delegated contract's verification logic.
Type: Standard | Status: Final
Authors: Vitalik Buterin; Sam Wilson; Ansgar Dietrichs; Ethereum Foundation
Algorithm Family: Classical (PQC-extensible)

chunks.title: NSA Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Cryptography
chunks.content:
Reference: NSA-QKD-Advisory-2023
Title: NSA Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Cryptography
Description: NSA advisory explaining why it does not recommend QKD for national security systems. States that QKD addresses only the key distribution problem, requires special-purpose equipment, introduces trusted node vulnerabilities, and has side-channel risks in real implementations. Recommends PQC (CNSA 2.0 algorithms) as the primary solution. Key reference for balanced QKD vs PQC evaluation.
Type: Guidance | Status: Published
Authors: National Security Agency (NSA)
Algorithm Family: QKD
Security Levels: All
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: Government;Defense;Finance
Region: Global
Published: 2023-10-24 | Updated: 2023-10-24

chunks.title: The Secure Shell (SSH) Transport Layer Protocol
chunks.content:
Reference: IETF RFC 4253
Title: The Secure Shell (SSH) Transport Layer Protocol
Description: Defines the SSH transport layer including key exchange, server authentication, and encryption. The key exchange phase is where QKD-derived pre-shared keys can be injected via the ext-info mechanism or future SSH KEX extensions. Foundational standard for understanding QKD integration into SSH.
Type: Protocol | Status: Standards Track RFC
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: SSH
Security Levels: All
Migration Urgency: Medium
Industries: All
Region: Global
Published: 2006-01-01 | Updated: 2006-01-01

chunks.title: IEEE 802.1AE-2018: MAC Security (MACsec)
chunks.content:
Reference: IEEE-802-1AE-2018
Title: IEEE 802.1AE-2018: MAC Security (MACsec)
Description: Defines MACsec — IEEE Layer 2 encryption standard for Ethernet networks. MACsec uses AES-GCM-128/256 for confidentiality and integrity. QKD-derived keys can be injected as Secure Association Keys (SAKs) via the MACsec Key Agreement (MKA) protocol, making MACsec a natural integration point for QKD in enterprise and telecom networks.
Type: Protocol | Status: IEEE Standard
Authors: IEEE 802.1 Working Group
Algorithm Family: AES-GCM; QKD integration
Security Levels: AES-128; AES-256
Migration Urgency: Medium
Industries: Finance;Government;Telecom;Enterprise
Region: Global
Published: 2006-08-01 | Updated: 2018-12-01

chunks.title: Use of Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms in JOSE
chunks.content:
Reference: draft-ietf-jose-pqc-kem
Title: Use of Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms in JOSE
Description: Defines how to use ML-KEM (FIPS 203) within JOSE/JWE for quantum-safe token encryption. Introduces alg identifiers ML-KEM-512, ML-KEM-768, ML-KEM-1024 for JWE key agreement, replacing quantum-vulnerable ECDH-ES. Companion to draft-ietf-jose-pqc-algorithms for signature migration.
Type: Standard (Draft) | Status: Internet-Draft
Authors: IETF JOSE WG
Algorithm Family: ML-KEM
Security Levels: L1;L3;L5
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global
Published: 2024-01-01 | Updated: 2025-11-01

chunks.title: Use of Post-Quantum Algorithms in JOSE
chunks.content:
Reference: draft-ietf-jose-pqc-algorithms
Title: Use of Post-Quantum Algorithms in JOSE
Description: Defines how to use ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) as digital signature algorithms in JOSE JWS. Specifies alg identifiers ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s and others for signing JWTs. The critical standard enabling PQC JWT signing across all JOSE implementations.

Authors: IETF JOSE WG

chunks.content:

Title: JSON Web Token Best Current Practices

chunks.title: JSON Web Token (JWT)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 7519
Title: JSON Web Token (JWT)
Description: Defines JWT as a compact URL-safe representation of claims between parties. Specifies the three-part structure (header.payload.signature), registered claim names (iss, sub, aud, exp, iat), and how JWS and JWE are used for signing and encryption. Foundation document for all JWT-based PQC migration work.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global

chunks.title: JSON Web Signature (JWS)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 7515
Title: JSON Web Signature (JWS)
Description: Defines the JWS standard for representing digitally signed content in JSON. Specifies the compact serialization (header.payload.signature) used in every signed JWT and the alg header parameter registry that PQC JOSE drafts extend with ML-DSA and SLH-DSA identifiers.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global
Published: 2015-05-01 | Updated: 2015-05-01

chunks.title: JSON Web Encryption (JWE)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 7516
Title: JSON Web Encryption (JWE)
Description: Defines the JWE standard for encrypting arbitrary content using JSON data structures. Specifies the 5-part compact serialization and key agreement mechanisms (ECDH-ES, RSA-OAEP) that ML-KEM replaces in PQC migration. Core reference for JWE encryption and PQC token confidentiality.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global
Published: 2015-05-01 | Updated: 2015-05-01

chunks.title: JSON Web Key (JWK)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 7517
Title: JSON Web Key (JWK)
Description: Defines the JWK format for representing cryptographic keys as JSON. JWKS endpoints publish public keys for JWT signature verification. PQC migration significantly increases JWKS response sizes: ML-DSA-65 public keys are 1952 bytes vs 65 bytes for P-256, challenging HTTP header limits and caching strategies.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global
Published: 2015-05-01 | Updated: 2015-05-01

chunks.title: JSON Web Algorithms (JWA)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 7518
Title: JSON Web Algorithms (JWA)
Description: Defines the algorithm registry for JOSE including RS256, ES256, ECDH-ES, AES-GCM, and HMAC. All currently registered signature and key agreement algorithms are quantum-vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. PQC JOSE drafts extend this registry with ML-DSA and ML-KEM algorithm identifiers.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs
Region: Global
Published: 2015-05-01 | Updated: 2015-05-01

chunks.title: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 6749
Title: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework
Description: Defines the OAuth 2.0 authorization framework enabling secure delegated access using access tokens. Access tokens and ID tokens issued by authorization servers are typically JWTs signed with RSA or ECDSA. PQC migration requires updating JWT signing across all OAuth authorization servers and resource server token validation.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: High
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs;Government
Region: Global
Published: 2012-10-01 | Updated: 2012-10-01

chunks.title: OAuth 2.0 Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP)
chunks.content:
Reference: RFC 9449
Title: OAuth 2.0 Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP)
Description: Defines DPoP for binding OAuth 2.0 tokens to a client public key using signed proof-of-possession JWTs. DPoP proof JWTs use ECDSA signatures that are quantum-vulnerable. Migration to ML-DSA-signed DPoP proofs requires JOSE PQC standardization and results in significantly larger proof headers per request.
Type: Standard | Status: Proposed Standard
Authors: IETF
Algorithm Family: 
Security Levels: 
Migration Urgency: Medium
Industries: IT;Finance;Identity Management;APIs;Government
Region: Global
Published: 2023-09-01 | Updated: 2023-09-01

chunks.title: ML-KEM-512
chunks.content:
Algorithm: ML-KEM-512
Family: KEM
Security Level: 1 (AES equivalent: AES-128)
Public Key Size: 800 bytes | Private Key Size: 1632 bytes
Signature/Ciphertext Size: 768 bytes
Shared Secret Size: 32 bytes
Performance: KeyGen 1x, Sign/Encaps 1x, Verify/Decaps 1x cycles
Stack RAM: ~4000 bytes
Optimization: Balanced
FIPS Standard: FIPS 203
Use Cases: Good for most applications; ~80x faster keygen than RSA-2048

chunks.title: ML-KEM-768
chunks.content:
Algorithm: ML-KEM-768
Family: KEM
Security Level: 3 (AES equivalent: AES-192)
Public Key Size: 1184 bytes | Private Key Size: 2400 bytes
Signature/Ciphertext Size: 1088 bytes
Shared Secret Size: 32 bytes
Performance: KeyGen 1.5x, Sign/Encaps 1.5x, Verify/Decaps 1.5x cycles
Stack RAM: ~6000 bytes
Optimization: Balanced
FIPS Standard: FIPS 203
Use Cases: Recommended general use; balances security and performance

chunks.title: ML-KEM-1024
chunks.content:
Algorithm: ML-KEM-1024
Family: KEM
Security Level: 5 (AES equivalent: AES-256)
Public Key Size: 1568 bytes | Private Key Size: 3168 bytes
Signature/Ciphertext Size: 1568 bytes
Shared Secret Size: 32 bytes
Performance: KeyGen 2x, Sign/Encaps 2x, Verify/Decaps 2x cycles
Stack RAM: ~8000 bytes

chunks.title: Aerospace / Aviation — Aircraft avionics cryptographic exposure: RTCA DO-326A defines airworthiness sec
chunks.content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Aircraft avionics cryptographic exposure: RTCA DO-326A defines airworthiness security processes for aircraft systems with 30-40 year service lives. ARINC 664 (AFDX) network security and flight management systems rely on RSA/ECDSA. Aircraft entering service today will operate well beyond CRQC arrival with no practical mid-life cryptographic retrofit path.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Avionics PKI, ARINC 664 network security, flight management system authentication
PQC Replacement: Crypto-agile avionics design, ML-DSA for flight software signing, hybrid certificate chains
Source: RTCA DO-326A / DO-356A Airworthiness Security

chunks.title: Aerospace / Aviation — Satellite communication HNDL vulnerability: Military and commercial satellites h
chunks.content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Satellite communication HNDL vulnerability: Military and commercial satellites have 15-25 year operational lifespans. NSA CNSA 2.0 mandates PQC for all national security systems including satellite uplink/downlink by 2035. Currently intercepted satellite communications are harvestable for future quantum decryption.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Satellite uplink/downlink RSA/ECDSA, ground station TLS, inter-satellite link encryption
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87 per CNSA 2.0, AES-256
Source: NSA CNSA 2.0 Cybersecurity Advisory

chunks.title: Aerospace / Aviation — Air traffic management authentication gap: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19 (2022
chunks.content:
Industry: Aerospace / Aviation
Threat: Air traffic management authentication gap: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19 (2022) addresses aviation cybersecurity but ADS-B transmissions remain unencrypted and unauthenticated. ADS-B currently lacks any cryptographic protection; quantum computing becomes relevant when authenticated ADS-B is deployed using public-key cryptography, which would itself require PQC to remain secure.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: ADS-B (unencrypted), Mode S, ATC data links, ACARS messaging
PQC Replacement: Authenticated ADS-B with ML-DSA, quantum-safe ATC protocols
Source: ICAO Assembly Resolution A41-19

chunks.title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — V2X PKI quantum transition: ISO/SAE 21434 (published August 2021) establishes cy
chunks.content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: V2X PKI quantum transition: ISO/SAE 21434 (published August 2021) establishes cybersecurity engineering requirements for road vehicles. V2X communications use ECDSA P-256 certificates per IEEE 1609.2. Vehicles have 12+ year lifecycles meaning 2026 production vehicles remain on roads through 2038+ when CRQCs may be operational.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: ECDSA P-256 V2X certificates, IEEE 1609.2 PKI, digital key infrastructure
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA V2X certificates, hybrid V2X authentication, FN-DSA for constrained ECUs
Source: ISO/SAE 21434 Road Vehicle Cybersecurity

chunks.title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — OTA firmware update signature forgery: Over-the-Air update mechanisms for modern
chunks.content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: OTA firmware update signature forgery: Over-the-Air update mechanisms for modern connected vehicles use RSA/ECDSA code signing. Quantum forgery of update signatures enables malicious code injection into vehicle ECUs controlling braking, steering, and powertrain systems.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA code signing, secure boot chains, ECU firmware verification
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA firmware signing, SLH-DSA for safety-critical ECUs, dual-signature verification
Source: SAE J3061 / ISO/SAE 21434 Vehicle Cybersecurity

chunks.title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — UNECE WP.29 R155/R156 cybersecurity regulation gap: Mandatory for new vehicle ty
chunks.content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: UNECE WP.29 R155/R156 cybersecurity regulation gap: Mandatory for new vehicle type approvals in EU since July 2022 and extended to all new vehicles July 2024. Requires cybersecurity management systems and secure OTA updates but does not yet address post-quantum cryptography creating a future compliance gap.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: TLS for telematics, RSA/ECDSA for OTA signing, connected car cloud API authentication
PQC Replacement: Hybrid TLS 1.3 with ML-KEM, ML-DSA OTA signing, crypto-agile telematics
Source: UNECE WP.29 Regulations R155/R156

chunks.title: Automotive / Connected Vehicles — In-vehicle network security quantum exposure: ISO/SAE 21434 covers the full vehi
chunks.content:
Industry: Automotive / Connected Vehicles
Threat: In-vehicle network security quantum exposure: ISO/SAE 21434 covers the full vehicle cybersecurity lifecycle including CAN bus, LIN, FlexRay, and automotive Ethernet backbone networks. Embedded ECUs have limited computational resources and constrained crypto-agility, making mid-lifecycle PQC migration impractical for vehicles already in production.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: CAN bus authentication, secure boot in ECUs, in-vehicle Ethernet encryption, HSM key storage
PQC Replacement: Hardware security modules with PQC support, crypto-agile ECU design, ML-DSA for secure boot
Source: ISO/SAE 21434 Road Vehicle Cybersecurity

chunks.title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud backup and disaster recovery HNDL risk: Air-gapped backups and long-term a
chunks.content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud backup and disaster recovery HNDL risk: Air-gapped backups and long-term archival storage encrypted with RSA/ECDSA key wrapping are vulnerable to harvest-now-decrypt-later. Cloud backup data often retained for 7+ years for compliance.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Backup encryption key wrapping, archival storage TLS, disaster recovery replication
PQC Replacement: PQC-enabled backup encryption, ML-KEM key wrapping, AES-256 for archival
Source: Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Guidelines

chunks.title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud Security Alliance quantum readiness guidance: CSA published quantum-safe s
chunks.content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud Security Alliance quantum readiness guidance: CSA published quantum-safe security guidance identifying crypto-agility as critical requirement for cloud deployments. Multi-cloud environments using 5+ key management systems face fragmented PQC migration paths.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Cloud KMS key wrapping, multi-cloud encryption, BYOK/HYOK solutions, secrets management
PQC Replacement: Unified PQC key management, ML-KEM cloud HSM integration, crypto-agile KMS
Source: Cloud Security Alliance Quantum-Safe Security

chunks.title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Federal cloud PQC compliance requirements: NIST SP 800-210 general access contro
chunks.content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Federal cloud PQC compliance requirements: NIST SP 800-210 general access control guidance for cloud systems combined with NIST IR 8547 deprecation timeline means federal cloud deployments must transition to PQC by 2030. FedRAMP and StateRAMP certifications will require FIPS 203/204/205 compliance.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Federal cloud TLS, FedRAMP-authorized encryption, cloud HSM FIPS modules
PQC Replacement: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM), FIPS 204 (ML-DSA), FIPS 205 (SLH-DSA) cloud implementations
Source: NIST IR 8547 / NIST SP 800-210

chunks.title: Cloud Computing / Data Centers — Cloud HSM and key management PQC gap: NIST SP 800-210 defines access control for
chunks.content:
Industry: Cloud Computing / Data Centers
Threat: Cloud HSM and key management PQC gap: NIST SP 800-210 defines access control for cloud systems but cloud-hosted HSMs and KMS services currently lack full PQC support. FIPS 140-3 validation for PQC modules is ongoing with multi-year certification timelines. Organizations using cloud HSMs face vendor dependency for PQC migration.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Cloud HSM root keys, KMS key wrapping, tenant isolation keys, FIPS 140-3 modules
PQC Replacement: FIPS 140-3 validated PQC HSM modules, bring-your-own-key PQC, ML-KEM key wrapping
Source: NIST SP 800-210 Cloud Access Control

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — Quantum-safe readiness gap: Organizations score average 25/100 on IBM Quantum-Sa
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: Quantum-safe readiness gap: Organizations score average 25/100 on IBM Quantum-Safe Readiness Index. 70% lack complete cryptographic inventory, creating massive vulnerability window. Estimated 5-6 year gap between CRQC arrival and organizational readiness completion.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: All asymmetric cryptography, PKI infrastructure, key management systems
PQC Replacement: Comprehensive CBOM, automated discovery tools, ML-KEM/ML-DSA migration
Source: IBM Institute for Business Value — Secure the Post-Quantum Future 2025

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — NIST IR 8547 proposed transition timeline: NIST Initial Public Draft (November 2
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: NIST IR 8547 proposed transition timeline: NIST Initial Public Draft (November 2024) establishes proposed timeline to deprecate RSA, ECDSA, ECDH, and EdDSA by 2030 and disallow them entirely after 2035. Sets 112-bit security minimum. Final version pending as of February 2026. Landmark publication making PQC migration a compliance requirement for federal systems.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/3072/4096, ECDSA P-256/P-384, ECDH, EdDSA
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-768/1024 (FIPS 203), ML-DSA-44/65/87 (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205)
Source: NIST IR 8547 Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography Standards

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — Vendor dependency crisis: 62% of organizations incorrectly assume vendors will m
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: Vendor dependency crisis: 62% of organizations incorrectly assume vendors will manage quantum-safe transition automatically. Lack of contractual PQC requirements and service-level agreements for crypto-agility.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Third-party API keys, vendor-managed HSMs, cloud KMS
PQC Replacement: Vendor PQC roadmap requirements, contractual crypto-agility clauses
Source: IBM Institute for Business Value — Secure the Post-Quantum Future 2025

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — CRQC timeline uncertainty: Global Risk Institute 2024 survey of 32 quantum exper
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: CRQC timeline uncertainty: Global Risk Institute 2024 survey of 32 quantum experts estimates 19-34% probability of a cryptographically relevant quantum computer (CRQC) within 10 years and 5-14% within 5 years. Most credible estimates place CRQC arrival in the 2030-2040 range with wide uncertainty. Harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks make immediate PQC migration urgent regardless of exact timeline.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096, ECC P-256/384, ECDSA, ECDH, EdDSA
PQC Replacement: Immediate hybrid PQC deployment, ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87
Source: Global Risk Institute Quantum Threat Timeline

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — FIPS 206 FN-DSA (Falcon) standardization: NIST submitted draft FIPS 206 for appr
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: FIPS 206 FN-DSA (Falcon) standardization: NIST submitted draft FIPS 206 for approval August 2025. FN-DSA is the 4th PQC algorithm to be standardized, selected for use cases where compact signature size is critical (root and intermediate certificates, constrained environments). Based on NTRU lattices using FFT. Expected IPD release for public comment followed by finalization in late 2026 or 2027.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA certificates requiring compact signatures, root CA keys, constrained device authentication
PQC Replacement: FN-DSA (FIPS 206) for compact signatures, ML-DSA for general-purpose signing
Source: NIST FIPS 206 FN-DSA Status

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — HQC selected as 5th PQC algorithm: NIST announced March 11 2025 the selection of
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: HQC selected as 5th PQC algorithm: NIST announced March 11 2025 the selection of HQC (Hamming Quasi-Cyclic) as a backup KEM to ML-KEM. HQC is based on error-correcting codes rather than lattices, providing algorithmic diversity in case a lattice-based vulnerability is discovered. Draft standard expected for public comment in early 2026 with final standard in 2027.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: All systems relying solely on ML-KEM without backup KEM
PQC Replacement: HQC as backup KEM alongside ML-KEM-768/1024, algorithm diversity strategy
Source: NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — EU coordinated PQC transition roadmap: European Commission published coordinated
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: EU coordinated PQC transition roadmap: European Commission published coordinated PQC roadmap (June 2025) establishing EU-wide milestones. By December 2026: national transition roadmaps and initial identification steps. By December 2030: high-risk use cases transitioned and PQC-by-default for new systems. By December 2035: full PQC transition complete across all EU member states.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: All quantum-vulnerable cryptography in EU member state systems and critical infrastructure
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, hybrid implementations per EU coordinated timeline
Source: European Commission Digital Strategy — PQC Roadmap

chunks.title: Cross-Industry — NIST SP 800-227 KEM recommendations: Published September 2025 as final guidance
chunks.content:
Industry: Cross-Industry
Threat: NIST SP 800-227 KEM recommendations: Published September 2025 as final guidance on key-encapsulation mechanisms. Provides definitions, security properties, and implementation recommendations for KEMs as companion guidance to FIPS 203 (ML-KEM). Establishes best practices for KEM usage in protocols, hybrid constructions, and key management — essential reference for correct PQC deployment.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: Incorrect or insecure KEM implementations, legacy key exchange protocols
PQC Replacement: FIPS 203 ML-KEM per SP 800-227 guidance, proper KEM-TLS integration
Source: NIST SP 800-227 Recommendations for KEMs

chunks.title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Bitcoin ECDSA transaction hijacking: Approximately $718B in quantum-vulnerable P
chunks.content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Bitcoin ECDSA transaction hijacking: Approximately $718B in quantum-vulnerable P2PK addresses with exposed public keys (price-dependent estimate). Early P2PK addresses including Satoshi's estimated 1.1M BTC are permanently vulnerable as public keys are exposed on-chain. P2PKH addresses are protected until first spend.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: secp256k1 ECDSA
PQC Replacement: P2QRH BIP proposal, Lamport signatures, hash-based migration
Source: Federal Reserve HNDL Paper

chunks.title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Ethereum Foundation PQC initiative: Dedicated post-quantum security team establi
chunks.content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Ethereum Foundation PQC initiative: Dedicated post-quantum security team established January 2026 with $2M in research prizes. Active development of account abstraction and Verkle tree migration paths. All Ethereum accounts that have transacted expose public keys making them quantum-vulnerable via secp256k1 ECDSA and BLS12-381.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: secp256k1 ECDSA, BLS12-381, keccak256 address derivation
PQC Replacement: Account Abstraction (EIP-4337), Verkle Trees, quantum-resistant signature schemes
Source: Ethereum Foundation PQC Program

chunks.title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Blockchain HNDL permanence risk: Federal Reserve research confirms distributed l
chunks.content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Blockchain HNDL permanence risk: Federal Reserve research confirms distributed ledger networks face permanent data privacy risks from harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks even with future PQC deployment. On-chain transaction data is immutable — encrypted data harvested today remains permanently exposed once CRQCs arrive.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048, ECDSA, ECDH in blockchain protocols, BLS signatures
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA, validator PQC authentication
Source: Federal Reserve Board FEDS Paper September 2025

chunks.title: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain — Cryptocurrency custody HSM quantum vulnerability: Institutional custody solution
chunks.content:
Industry: Cryptocurrency / Blockchain
Threat: Cryptocurrency custody HSM quantum vulnerability: Institutional custody solutions managing billions in digital assets rely on secp256k1 ECDSA keys stored in HSMs. NIST PQC standardization identifies ECDSA as quantum-vulnerable. Custody HSM vendors face multi-year FIPS 140-3 recertification timelines for PQC-enabled modules, creating a gap between threat emergence and mitigation availability.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: secp256k1 ECDSA custody keys, RSA/ECDH key wrapping, HSM root keys
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, PQC-enabled custody HSMs
Source: Cryptocurrency Security Standard (CCSS)

chunks.title: Energy / Critical Infrastructure — Power grid SCADA quantum vulnerability: IEC 62351 specifies security for power s
chunks.content:
Industry: Energy / Critical Infrastructure
Threat: Power grid SCADA quantum vulnerability: IEC 62351 specifies security for power system communications (DNP3, IEC 61850, IEC 60870-5-104) using RSA/ECDSA for authentication. Critical infrastructure with 20-30 year equipment lifecycles and limited OTA update capability creates massive quantum exposure window.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048 in SCADA authentication, ECDSA in IEC 61850, DNP3 Secure Authentication
PQC Replacement: IEC 62351 quantum update, ML-DSA for SCADA authentication, hybrid protocols
Source: IEC 62351 Power Systems Security Standards

chunks.title: Energy / Critical Infrastructure — Nuclear facility digital I&C quantum exposure: NRC 10 CFR 73.54 requires cyberse
chunks.content:

Threat: Nuclear facility digital I&C quantum exposure: NRC 10 CFR 73.54 requires cybersecurity for nuclear power plant digital instrumentation and control systems. Nuclear plants operate under 60+ year licenses (80-year extensions approved). Digital safety systems deployed today will operate well into the CRQC era.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Safety system digital I&C cryptography, nuclear safeguards data encryption
PQC Replacement: Defense-in-depth with ML-KEM/ML-DSA, CNSA 2.0 compliance for nuclear systems
Source: NRC Cybersecurity Requirements (10 CFR 73.54)

chunks.title: Energy / Critical Infrastructure — Smart grid quantum security research: University of Toronto CA$1.45M research pr
chunks.content:
Industry: Energy / Critical Infrastructure

Over 1 billion smart meters globally will require PQC migration; many lack OTA update capability requiring physical replacement.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: AMI mesh networking, smart meter PKI, PMU authentication, distribution automation
PQC Replacement: Quantum-safe AMI protocols, IEC 62351 PQC update, constrained device PQC
Source: University of Toronto Smart Grid Security Research

chunks.title: Energy / Critical Infrastructure — Pipeline and oil/gas SCADA quantum vulnerability: IEC 62443 governs industrial a
chunks.content:
Industry: Energy / Critical Infrastructure
Threat: Pipeline and oil/gas SCADA quantum vulnerability: IEC 62443 governs industrial automation security for natural gas, oil, and water pipeline control systems. Equipment lifecycles of 20-30 years and air-gapped or semi-air-gapped network architectures complicate PQC migration. Pipeline SCADA systems are classified as critical infrastructure by CISA.

Cryptography at Risk: ICS/SCADA protocols, VPN encryption for pipeline monitoring, RTU authentication
PQC Replacement: IEC 62443 PQC update, hybrid VPN protocols, ML-DSA for RTU authentication
Source: IEC 62443 Industrial Automation Security

chunks.title: Financial Services / Banking — BIS Project Leap quantum-safe payment system cryptography: Phase 2 launched July
chunks.content:

Threat: BIS Project Leap quantum-safe payment system cryptography: Phase 2 launched July 2025 with Bank of Italy, Bank of France, Deutsche Bundesbank, Nexi-Colt, and SWIFT testing hybrid PQC on TARGET2 real-time gross settlement system. Demonstrates quantum-safe cryptographic integration for cross-border payment infrastructure processing trillions daily.

Cryptography at Risk: Payment system infrastructure, cross-border settlement, RSA-2048, ECDSA, TLS legacy
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, hybrid TLS 1.3, HQC

chunks.title: Financial Services / Banking — Harvest Now Decrypt Later (HNDL) attacks targeting long-lived financial data inc
chunks.content:
Industry: Financial Services / Banking
Threat: Harvest Now Decrypt Later (HNDL) attacks targeting long-lived financial data including transaction records and settlement logs. Federal Reserve research confirms cryptocurrency networks face permanent data privacy risks from HNDL even with future PQC deployment. Financial records retained for regulatory compliance spanning decades are prime targets.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048, ECDSA, ECDH, ECC-256/384
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA, AES-256
Source: Federal Reserve Board FEDS Paper September 2025

chunks.title: Financial Services / Banking — G7 Cyber Expert Group PQC roadmap: January 2026 statement coordinated by U.S. Tr
chunks.content:
Industry: Financial Services / Banking
Threat: G7 Cyber Expert Group PQC roadmap: January 2026 statement coordinated by U.S. Treasury and Bank of England establishes G7-wide framework for financial sector quantum-safe migration. Calls for cryptographic inventory, vendor roadmap alignment, and coordinated transition planning across G7 financial systems.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: G7 financial infrastructure, cross-border payment systems, correspondent banking TLS
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, hybrid implementations, coordinated G7 PQC standards
Source: G7 Cyber Expert Group Statement (U.S. Treasury)

chunks.title: Financial Services / Banking — HSM backup key extraction vulnerability: Master encryption keys wrapped with RSA
chunks.content:
Industry: Financial Services / Banking
Threat: HSM backup key extraction vulnerability: Master encryption keys wrapped with RSA in HSM backup archives become recoverable with quantum computers, exposing entire key hierarchies protecting decades of financial data. NIST SP 800-227 (September 2025) provides formal KEM guidance for transitioning key wrapping to quantum-safe mechanisms.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: RSA key wrapping in HSM backups, ECDH key agreement, archived master keys
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024 per NIST SP 800-227, AES-256-GCM key wrapping
Source: NIST SP 800-227 KEM Recommendations

chunks.title: Financial Services / Banking — FS-ISAC PQC migration urgency warning: Financial Services Information Sharing an
chunks.content:
Industry: Financial Services / Banking
Threat: FS-ISAC PQC migration urgency warning: Financial Services Information Sharing and Analysis Center (September 2025) warns that financial sector organizations have not defined or allocated resources for quantum-resistant migration, compressing transition into unrealistically short timeframes. Without immediate action, roadmap collapse by 2030 compliance deadlines is likely.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA, ECC, TLS 1.2/1.3 key exchange, PKI certificates across financial infrastructure
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, hybrid implementations, immediate cryptographic inventory
Source: FS-ISAC — The Timeline for Post Quantum Cryptographic Migration

chunks.title: Government / Defense — HNDL on classified data: TOP SECRET information requiring 25-75+ year protection
chunks.content:
Industry: Government / Defense
Threat: HNDL on classified data: TOP SECRET information requiring 25-75+ year protection is actively harvested for future quantum decryption. Current encryption provides no protection against this threat.

Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096, ECDH P-384, ECDSA
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87, AES-256
Source: NSA CNSA 2.0 Cybersecurity Advisory

chunks.title: Government / Defense — CNSA 2.0 compliance deadline pressure: NSA mandates CNSA 2.0 compliance with pha
chunks.content:
Industry: Government / Defense
Threat: CNSA 2.0 compliance deadline pressure: NSA mandates CNSA 2.0 compliance with phased deadlines — software/firmware signing supported and preferred by 2025 (exclusively by 2030), networking equipment supported and preferred by 2026 (exclusively by 2030), NSS acquisitions by January 2027, web browsers/servers/cloud supported and preferred by 2025 (exclusively by 2033), full transition by 2035.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: Federal PKI, NSS communications, classified systems, defense supply chain
PQC Replacement: FIPS 140-3 validated PQC modules, CMVP certification acceleration
Source: NSA CNSA 2.0 Guidance

chunks.title: Government / Defense — Federal PQC procurement mandate: CISA January 2026 federal buying guidance pursu
chunks.content:
Industry: Government / Defense
Threat: Federal PQC procurement mandate: CISA January 2026 federal buying guidance pursuant to Executive Order 14306 (June 2025) requires agencies to procure only quantum-resistant technology across designated product categories including cloud services, collaboration tools, web browsers/servers, and endpoint security. Vendors without PQC FIPS validation risk federal contract exclusion.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: COTS products, federal IT infrastructure, defense systems
PQC Replacement: NIST FIPS 203/204/205 compliance, ACVP testing, vendor PQC roadmaps
Source: CISA Product Categories for PQC Technologies

chunks.title: Government / Defense — Federal PKI signature forgery risk: Quantum-enabled impersonation of federal sys
chunks.content:
Industry: Government / Defense
Threat: Federal PKI signature forgery risk: Quantum-enabled impersonation of federal systems and fraudulent certificate issuance affecting government-wide trust infrastructure. The Federal PKI serves as the root of trust for digital identity across federal agencies, and quantum forgery of CA certificates would compromise the entire federal trust hierarchy.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048/4096 in Federal PKI, ECDSA P-256/P-384 intermediate certificates
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA, hybrid X.509 certificates
Source: Federal PKI Policy Authority

chunks.title: Government / Defense — Nuclear command and control quantum exposure: NC3 (Nuclear Command, Control, and
chunks.content:
Industry: Government / Defense
Threat: Nuclear command and control quantum exposure: NC3 (Nuclear Command, Control, and Communications) systems operate on 50+ year lifecycles and represent the highest-consequence quantum threat scenario. NSA CNSA 2.0 specifically identifies national security systems including strategic communications as priority for PQC transition.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: All public-key cryptography in NC3 systems, strategic communications encryption
PQC Replacement: CNSA 2.0 full suite, AES-256, ML-KEM-1024, ML-DSA-87
Source: NSA CNSA 2.0 Cybersecurity Advisory

chunks.title: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical — HNDL on patient records: Healthcare data has lifetime sensitivity; genomic data
chunks.content:

Cryptography at Risk: RSA, ECDH, ECDSA, TLS
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-768/1024, ML-DSA, AES-256
Source: HHS HIPAA Security Rule

chunks.title: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical — Healthcare cloud encryption and breach crisis: Only 4% of healthcare operators e
chunks.content:
Industry: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical
Threat: Healthcare cloud encryption and breach crisis: Only 4% of healthcare operators encrypted 80%+ of sensitive cloud data. Thales 2025 report shows 57% evaluating PQC but low actual deployment. 677 major breaches in 2024 exposed 182M+ individuals globally. Health records retain lifetime sensitivity making them prime HNDL targets.

Cryptography at Risk: EHR databases, medical imaging, patient portals, health information exchanges, cloud encryption
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM-768/1024, ML-DSA, AES-256-GCM, quantum-safe TLS
Source: Thales 2025 Data Threat Report Healthcare Edition

chunks.title: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical — FDA premarket cybersecurity guidance: FDA finalized guidance (September 2023) un
chunks.content:
Industry: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical
Threat: FDA premarket cybersecurity guidance: FDA finalized guidance (September 2023) under Section 524B of FD&C Act (Consolidated Appropriations Act 2023). Requires cryptographic risk assessment and Software Bill of Materials for new device submissions. Medical devices with 10-20 year lifecycles deploying now will operate into the CRQC era.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA-2048, ECDSA in device firmware, TLS in medical devices, PKI for device authentication

Source: FDA Premarket Cybersecurity Guidance 2023

chunks.title: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical — Connected medical device lifecycle quantum exposure: FDA Section 524B of FD&C Ac
chunks.content:
Industry: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical
Threat: Connected medical device lifecycle quantum exposure: FDA Section 524B of FD&C Act requires cybersecurity for medical devices. Implantable and connected devices (pacemakers, insulin pumps, remote monitoring) with 10-20 year lifecycles being deployed today will operate well into the CRQC era. Limited computational resources and power constraints in implantable devices restrict PQC options.
Criticality: Critical
Cryptography at Risk: BLE cryptography, TLS in medical IoT, embedded ECC in implantable devices
PQC Replacement: Crypto-agile device design, hybrid firmware updates, ML-DSA for device authentication
Source: FDA Cybersecurity for Medical Devices

chunks.title: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical — Drug supply chain authentication quantum risk: Drug Supply Chain Security Act (D
chunks.content:
Industry: Healthcare / Pharmaceutical
Threat: Drug supply chain authentication quantum risk: Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) mandates product serialization and verification to prevent counterfeit drug infiltration. EPCIS (Electronic Product Code Information Services) and verification systems rely on digital signatures and TLS. Quantum forgery of verification responses enables counterfeit drug injection into the legitimate supply chain.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: EPCIS digital signatures, TLS authentication for verification systems, manufacturer PKI
PQC Replacement: ML-DSA for supply chain signing, hybrid verification certificates, PQC-enabled EPCIS
Source: FDA Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA)

chunks.title: Insurance — NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law (MDL-668) quantum exposure: Adopted 2017
chunks.content:

Threat: NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law (MDL-668) quantum exposure: Adopted 2017 and enacted in 25+ states, requires encryption of nonpublic information in transit and at rest. Actuarial models, claims history spanning decades, and underwriting algorithms represent long-lived sensitive data prime for HNDL attacks.
Criticality: High
Cryptography at Risk: RSA/ECDSA protecting policyholder data, TLS for claims processing, database encryption
PQC Replacement: ML-KEM for key exchange, AES-256-GCM, ML-DSA for document signing
Source: NAIC Insurance Data Security Model Law (MDL-668)

chunks.content:

Threat: New York DFS cybersecurity regulation (23 NYCRR 500) quantum gap: Requires risk assessment and encryption for financial services including insurance companies. Life insurance and annuity records with 50+ year retention periods are prime HNDL targets. Regulation does not yet address post-quantum cryptography.

Cryptography at Risk: Database encryption, policyholder data TLS, claims management systems
PQC Replacement: PQC-enabled encryption, hybrid TLS 1.3, ML-KEM key management
Source: NY DFS Cybersecurity Regulation (23 NYCRR 500)

chunks.title: Insurance — Systemic cyber risk to insurance sector: Geneva Association research identifies
chunks.content:
Industry: Insurance
Threat: Systemic cyber risk to insurance sector: Geneva Association research identifies systemic cyber risk including quantum threats to insurance industry managing multi-decade policy data. Life insurance and pension records retain sensitivity for 50+ years. Reinsurance treaties with multi-year duration vulnerable to data manipulation.
